questions.question: Explain the difference between strlen() and sizeof() when applied to a string.
keyPoints: strlen() returns number of characters excluding null terminator
sizeof() returns total memory allocated including null terminator
strlen() is a function from string.h, sizeof is an operator
Example: "Hello" has strlen=5 but sizeof=6 in a properly sized array

questions.question: Write a C program to count the number of vowels and consonants in a string.
keyPoints: Accept string input from user
Iterate through each character
Check if character is vowel (a,e,i,o,u,A,E,I,O,U)
Use isalpha() to check if character is a letter
Increment appropriate counter

questions.question: What is the difference between gets() and fgets() functions? Which one is safer and why?
keyPoints: gets() has no buffer overflow protection
fgets() allows specifying maximum characters to read
fgets() includes newline character in input
fgets() is safer because it prevents buffer overflow attacks
gets() is deprecated and removed from C11 standard

questions.question: Explain the concept of a 2D array of strings in C with an example declaration.
keyPoints: 2D array of strings is an array of character arrays
Declaration: char names[5][20] stores 5 strings of max 19 characters
Each row represents one string
Memory layout: contiguous blocks of characters
Example: char cities[3][10] = {"Delhi", "Mumbai", "Chennai"}

questions.question: What is the purpose of character classification functions in ctype.h? Give examples.
keyPoints: Used to check properties of characters
isalpha() checks for alphabetic characters
isdigit() checks for numeric digits
isalnum() checks for alphanumeric characters
isspace() checks for whitespace characters
toupper() and tolower() convert case

questions.question: Explain what happens when strcpy() is used with overlapping strings.
keyPoints: Behavior is undefined for overlapping strings
Use memmove() for overlapping strings
strcpy() assumes destination is large enough
Always ensure destination buffer has sufficient size
Undefined behavior can cause memory corruption

questions.question: Write a C program to check whether a given string is a palindrome or not.
keyPoints: Palindrome: reads same forwards and backwards
Algorithm: Use two pointers (start and end)
Compare characters at start and end positions
Move start forward, end backward
If all pairs match, string is palindrome
Use strlen() to find length
Handle case sensitivity consideration
Example: "madam", "racecar", "level"

questions.question: Write a C program to reverse a string without using the strrev() function.
keyPoints: Cannot use built-in strrev() function
Method 1: Swap characters from beginning and end
Method 2: Use an auxiliary character array
Calculate string length using strlen()
Use two indices: i starting from 0, j from length-1
Swap arr[i] and arr[j] until i >= j
Alternative: Copy characters in reverse to new array

questions.question: Explain string manipulation functions from string.h with examples of their usage.
keyPoints: strlen(): returns length of string
strcpy(dest, source): copies source to destination
strcat(dest, source): concatenates source to dest
strcmp(s1, s2): compares two strings
strrev(): reverses string (non-standard)
strupr(): converts to uppercase
strlwr(): converts to lowercase
All require string.h header
Each has specific return values and parameters

questions.question: Write a C program to sort an array of strings in alphabetical order.
keyPoints: Use 2D character array to store strings
Apply sorting algorithm (selection sort or bubble sort)
Use strcmp() for comparing strings
Use strcpy() or strdup() for swapping
Algorithm: Compare adjacent strings, swap if out of order
Continue until entire array is sorted
Handle case sensitivity consideration
Print sorted strings
